# Arca Kotlin SDK — AI Context

> com.github.arcaresearch:arca-kotlin-sdk — Kotlin/Android SDK for the Arca
> financial infrastructure platform (accounts, payments, perpetuals trading,
> real-time streaming, audit trails). Hand-written port of the Swift SDK; full
> feature parity. Targets JVM 1.8 bytecode (Android minSdk 24+).

```kotlin
// settings.gradle.kts: repositories { maven { url = uri("https://jitpack.io") } }
dependencies { implementation("com.github.arcaresearch:arca-kotlin-sdk:v0.1.0") }
```

Package is `network.arca.sdk`; the published Maven artifactId is `arca-sdk`.
Most calls are extension functions on the `Arca` class (`import network.arca.sdk.*`).

## Conventions (Kotlin-specific)

- Reads are `suspend` functions; mutations return a handle immediately (request runs in the background coroutine scope).
- Money/amount values are decimal strings ("50", "0.01").
- Market ids are canonical `{exchange}:{id}` ("hl:0:BTC", "hl:1:TSLA"), case-sensitive.
- Market-data timestamps are Unix epoch ms; all others are RFC 3339 UTC strings.
- Errors are a sealed `ArcaException`: Validation, Unauthorized, Forbidden, NotFound(code),
  Conflict(code), Internal, Exchange(code), Network(cause), Decoding(cause),
  NonJsonResponse(statusCode, body), OperationFailed(operation), Unknown(code). Branch with `when`.
- Client-side only: authenticate with a scoped JWT (never embed an API key in an app).
- Always call `arca.close()` (e.g. ViewModel.onCleared) to tear down the socket + coroutine scope.
- Token-provider clients refresh ~30s before expiry, retry once on 401, and retry once on 403
  (`FORBIDDEN` / `REALM_SCOPE_MISMATCH` — cached token valid but minted for a different identity,
  e.g. after a signed-in-user switch; a 403-triggered refresh also forces the live WebSocket to
  reconnect). `onAuthError` fires when a 401/403 survives the refresh — tear down and rebuild.
- An `Arca` instance is bound to the identity its tokens are minted for: on identity change call
  `close()` and build a fresh instance with the new user's provider.

## Initialization

```kotlin
// Scoped JWT (realm read from claims)
val arca = Arca(token = jwt)
// With explicit realm + auto-refresh
val arca = Arca(token = jwt, realmId = "my-realm", tokenProvider = { fetchToken() })
// Provider only (suspend; fetches the first token now)
val arca = Arca.withTokenProvider(tokenProvider = { fetchToken() }, realmId = "my-realm")
```

## Operation & order handles

Mutations return `OperationHandle<R>` (HTTP runs in background):
- `submitted(): R`            — HTTP response before settlement (may be pending)
- `settled(): R` / `settle()` — suspends until terminal; throws ArcaException.OperationFailed on failed/expired
- `settled(timeoutSeconds: Double)` — throws ArcaException.Unknown("TIMEOUT") past the deadline

`placeOrder(...)` returns `OrderHandle` (wraps OperationHandle):
- `filled(timeoutSeconds = 30.0): SimOrderWithFills`
- `fills(timeoutSeconds = 300.0): Flow<SimFill>` — closes on terminal status
- `cancel(path)` / `resize(newSize, path)` (sized orders only)

## Intent -> method

| Intent | Method |
|---|---|
| Create wallet | arca.ensureDenominatedArca(ref) |
| Create any object | arca.ensureArca(ref, type) |
| Delete object | arca.ensureDeleted(ref, sweepTo?, liquidatePositions?) |
| Get object | arca.getObject(path) |
| Balances | arca.getBalances(id) / getBalancesByPath(path) |
| Transfer | arca.transfer(path, from, to, amount) |
| Fund (dev) | arca.fundAccount(arcaRef, amount) |
| Withdraw (dev) | arca.defundAccount(arcaPath, amount) |
| Payment link | arca.createPaymentLink(type, arcaRef, amount) -> resp.paymentLink.url |
| Idempotency nonce | arca.nonce(path) |
| Aggregation | arca.getPathAggregation(path, asOf?) |
| Equity history | arca.getEquityHistory(path, from, to, points) |
| P&L history | arca.getPnlHistory(path, from, to, points) |
| Operation detail | arca.getOperation(id) |
| Create exchange | arca.ensurePerpsExchange(ref) |
| Place order | arca.placeOrder(path, objectId, market, side, orderType, size, price?, leverage?, ...) |
| Cancel / close | arca.cancelOrder(...) / arca.closePosition(...) |
| Position TP/SL | arca.setStopLoss / setTakeProfit / setPositionTpsl / clearPositionTpsl |
| Open with atomic bracket | arca.openWithBracket(...) -> entry + takeProfit?/stopLoss? handles |
| Exchange state | arca.getExchangeState(id) |
| Active asset data | arca.getActiveAssetData(objectId, market, ...) |
| Set leverage / margin | arca.updateLeverage / updateIsolatedMargin / setMarginMode |
| Market meta | arca.getMarketMeta() / market(id) / resolveMarkets(symbol, opts) |
| Mids/tickers/book | arca.getMarketMids / getMarketTickers / getOrderBook |
| Candles | arca.getCandles(market, interval, options) |
| OI history | arca.getOIHistory(market, interval, options) -> OHLC open interest + 24h ntlVlm + mark; intervals 1m/5m/1h/1d |

## Watch streams (real-time)

Each factory opens the shared WebSocket and returns a stream exposing a `StateFlow`
snapshot + a `Flow` of `updates`; call `ready()` to suspend until the first snapshot
and `stop()` when done.

Snapshot-typed `updates` flows (prices, valuations, aggregation, exchange state,
max order size, equity/P&L/candle charts) replay the latest snapshot to collectors
that attach late, and drop intermediate snapshots for slow collectors (latest-only).
Event-typed `updates` flows (operations, balances, fills, funding, raw candles,
open-interest bars) do not replay; collect before mutating to observe every event.

Equity/P&L chart streams append an SDK-synthesized live tip derived from the live
aggregation (equity incl. unrealized P&L); it always carries
`status = ChartPointStatus.OPEN` so it can be identified (restyled / dropped /
re-anchored) without timestamp inference. Historical points come from the server
projection and carry the server's own status (`sealed`/`carried`/`incomplete`).

| Method | Snapshot / updates |
|---|---|
| arca.watchPrices() | StateFlow<Map<String,String>> prices |
| arca.watchObject(path) | StateFlow<ObjectValuation?> valuation |
| arca.watchObjects(paths) | StateFlow<Map<String,ObjectValuation>> valuations |
| arca.watchOperations(path) | Flow<Pair<Operation, RealmEvent>> |
| arca.watchExchangeState(id) | StateFlow<ExchangeState?> |
| arca.watchFills(id) | Flow<Pair<Fill, RealmEvent>> |
| arca.watchFunding(id) | Flow<Pair<FundingPayment, EventEnvelope>> |
| arca.watchEquityChart(path, from, to) | StateFlow<List<EquityPoint>> + EquityChartUpdate |
| arca.watchPnlChart(path, from, to) | StateFlow<List<PnlPoint>> + PnlChartUpdate |
| arca.watchCandleChart(market, interval, ...) | StateFlow<List<Candle>> + CandleChartUpdate |
| arca.watchOI(coins, intervals) | Flow<OIEvent> (market, interval, bar, isClosed) |
| arca.watchMaxOrderSize(options) | StateFlow<ActiveAssetData?> |

Lower-level: `arca.ws` is the WebSocketManager (events flow, typed event flows,
onGap, onResume, onAuthenticated, ensureConnected, disconnect).

## Anti-patterns

1. Reuse a nonce path on retry; do not call nonce() inside a retry loop.
2. Use canonical market ids ("hl:0:BTC", never "BTC"); resolve a display symbol via resolveMarkets(symbol).
3. fundAccount/defundAccount are dev-only; use createPaymentLink in production.
4. size is base-asset units, not USD. Min notional is $10.
5. Always close() the Arca instance (and stop() each watch) to release the socket + scope.

## TP/SL is an OCO bracket; openWithBracket is atomic

setPositionTpsl stamps one auto-generated ocoGroupId on both legs; when either fills
(even partially) the venue cancels the other (cancelReason="sibling_filled"). A TP/SL
leg closes the WHOLE position by default (sizeToMax); set a base-unit size for a
reduce-only partial (min-notional waived) — when sized, setPositionTpsl does NOT
auto-link the legs. openWithBracket(...) opens a position and attaches its reduce-only
TP/SL triggers as one atomic, all-or-nothing batch; the trigger legs arm only when the
entry fills, and it returns one OrderHandle per leg.
